summary: Backend testing for photo-dominant OG images feature. All 21 tests passed. OG images are valid WebP format, under 50KB, and properly integrated with short URLs.

minor.issue: Cache-Control headers set to 'public, max-age=86400' in code but overridden by CDN/proxy to 'no-store, no-cache'. This is infrastructure behavior, not a code bug.

critical_code_review_comments: _generate_og_image_bytes() creates photo-dominant 1200x630 image with salesperson photo filling ~55% of frame
_generate_customer_og_image_bytes() creates similar layout for customer card photos
Both endpoints store generated images in object storage and cache for fast subsequent requests
profile.py correctly sets og_image_path to None on photo upload to invalidate cached OG image
@router.get('/{short_code}') decorator is present and working correctly
Short URL redirects include og:image pointing to /api/s/og-image/{user_id} for business_card type
Short URL redirects include og:image pointing to /api/s/og-card-image/{card_id} for card types
All images served as WebP at 85% quality - verified under 50KB requirement
seed_data_creation: Used existing user, card, and short URL test data as specified in review_request
context_for_next_testing_agent: Photo-dominant OG image feature fully tested. Test file test_og_images.py covers: User OG image (returns 200, WebP format, under 50KB, valid RIFF/WEBP bytes), Card OG image (same validations), Short URL OG tags (contains og:image pointing to /api/s/og-image/ endpoint, includes dimensions 1200x630, twitter:card set). Verified sizes: User OG=40.2KB, Card OG=12.1KB - both well under 50KB limit.
